danger_signals.visual_form: 2D sprite enemies rendered at fixed angles; distinctive demonic/undead silhouettes
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; indicates need for weapon readiness and evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Pits of toxic waste, lowering ceilings, environmental damage zones
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial threat; player must navigate carefully or use protective equipment

danger_signals.visual_form: Sealed doors requiring keycards or switches; visual obstruction
danger_signals.meaning: Progression blocked; signals need to acquire access items or solve environmental puzzles

danger_signals.visual_form: Increased movement speed and attack frequency on higher difficulty settings
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level; enemy aggression scales with difficulty

danger_signals.visual_form: UI indicators showing depleted health and armor points
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability; indicates need to locate health packs or retreat

danger_signals.visual_form: Final level culminates in distinctive boss enemy sprite
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat event; climactic danger requiring defeat to progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible weapons and ammunition scattered throughout levels or dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Player empowerment; indicates capability to defend against threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Health packs and armor items placed in accessible locations
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; player can restore vitality and defensive capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: Invulnerability spheres, invisibility power-ups, berserker packs, radiation suits
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary player enhancement; signals opportunity for tactical advantage or hazard mitigation

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated exit areas visible to player navigation
safety_signals.meaning: Level completion path; signals progression possibility and safe escape from current area
Navigation aid; reduces disorientation and helps player identify safe paths

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear visual field without visible demon/undead sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary respite; indicates momentary safety window for resource gathering

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Military gear, space marine armor, weapons cache
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases on Mars moons; human technology infrastructure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Demonic morphology; horns, claws, otherworldly appearance; distinct sprite designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Possessed undead humans (zombies, shotgun-wielding undead soldiers); pure demons (Imps, Barons of Hell, Cyberdemons)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hell dimension terrain; toxic waste; corrupted architectural spaces; demonic symbols and infernal landscape features

faction_indicators.color_identity: Decayed flesh tones, corrupted military uniform colors
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Undead soldier sprites with recognizable human silhouette; retain weapon shapes (shotguns, pistols)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Zombies, undead soldiers; visually distinguish from pure demons through retained human proportions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases; evidence of human occupation corrupted by demonic presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasing enemy variety, density, and spawn patterns; more powerful demon types appear in later levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy movement and attack speed increase; enemies coordinate in larger groups; boss encounters escalate in complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Toxic waste pits rendered as distinctive colored liquid zones; radiation suit power-ups become necessary resources
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must avoid or protect against hazard damage; navigation patterns shift to avoid contaminated areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Early levels (Knee-Deep in the Dead) show Mars military bases; walls feature military design; undead soldiers indicate human casualties
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player navigates abandoned/corrupted military facility; encounters both human-origin undead and pure demons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Later episodes feature increasingly demonic architecture; hellish terrain replaces military infrastructure; color palette shifts to infernal reds/blacks
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies become exclusively demonic; navigation becomes more chaotic and less structured than military base layout

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon and ammo distribution varies by level; some areas feature generous supplies, others require careful rationing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player adapts weapon selection and engagement distance based on available resources

environmental_storytelling.example: Military base layouts with barracks, command centers, and storage facilities establish human occupation before demon invasion
Undead soldiers with weapons indicate human defenders who lost the initial conflict; progression from possessed humans to pure demons shows invasion escalation
Toxic waste pits and corrupted facilities indicate demonic contamination and environmental damage from invasion
Episode structure (Knee-Deep in the Dead → Hell Unleashed → Inferno) shows journey from Mars military bases through gateway to hell dimension

environmental_storytelling.technique: Resource placement as player capability signal
environmental_storytelling.example: Boss levels feature enhanced weapons (BFG 9000, plasma rifle availability) before major encounters, preparing player for escalated threat
Military base modernist design contrasts sharply with organic, demonic hellscape architecture, visually communicating faction territory and control

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Move toward player position; attack using appropriate method (biting, clawing, magic fireball attacks)
Stationary or patrolling; will activate upon visual or auditory detection
Use ranged weapons (shotguns, pistols); maintain distance and fire patterns
Can engage enemies directly; use melee weapons; absorb damage
Seeks cover; prioritizes health/armor pickups; uses ranged weapons at distance; avoids direct engagement
Increased aggression, complex attack patterns, significant health pool; requires sustained engagement or tactical weapon use
Swarm behavior creates emergent threat; player must manage multiple simultaneous attack vectors

notes: Doom (1993) communicates world-state primarily through enemy composition, environmental architecture, and hazard presence. The two-tier faction system (Human military vs. Demon invasion) is expressed through sprite design, environmental markers, and spatial layout. Danger is signaled through visible enemy proximity, hazard visibility, and resource depletion mechanics. Safety emerges from weapon availability, health resources, and clear exit paths. The game uses no dynamic faction reputation or political dialogue systems; all communication is spatial and visual. Difficulty settings provide scalable threat manifestation through enemy behavior modification rather than environmental change.
